name: Data Entry Clerk
use: Move into data QA, process design or exception-handling
what: Manual keying of data into systems — one of the first roles to be fully automated because the task is repetitive and rule-based.
how: 1) Documents digitised → 2) OCR reads them → 3) AI validates fields → 4) auto-posts to systems → 5) humans only handle exceptions.
usage: Replacement tools: OCR + RPA (UiPath), LLM extraction
future: Human fallback: Move into data QA, process design or exception-handling

name: Basic Bookkeeping
use: Shift to advisory accounting, tax strategy, CFO-style work
what: Routine transaction recording and reconciliation — highly structured, so AI accounting tools now do most of it automatically.
how: 1) Bank feeds auto-import → 2) AI categorises → 3) auto-reconciles → 4) flags anomalies → 5) accountant reviews only edge cases.
usage: Replacement tools: QuickBooks AI, Xero, Ramp
future: Human fallback: Shift to advisory accounting, tax strategy, CFO-style work

name: Telemarketing / Cold Calling
use: Move to relationship sales, closing, account management
what: Scripted outbound calls — conversational AI voices now handle first-contact calls at scale.
how: 1) AI dials lists → 2) natural-voice AI pitches → 3) qualifies interest → 4) books meetings → 5) warm leads passed to humans.
usage: Replacement tools: AI voice agents, dialers
future: Human fallback: Move to relationship sales, closing, account management

name: Basic Content Moderation
use: Specialise in policy, appeals, trust & safety strategy
what: First-pass filtering of text/images — AI classifiers now catch the clear cases far faster than humans.
how: 1) AI scans uploads → 2) auto-removes clear violations → 3) scores grey areas → 4) escalates hard calls → 5) humans judge nuance.
future: Human fallback: Specialise in policy, appeals, trust & safety strategy

name: Customer Support Agent
use: Move to complex support, success management, escalations
what: Tier-1 support — AI agents resolve common issues instantly, shrinking (not erasing) human teams to complex cases.
how: 1) AI chatbot handles FAQs → 2) resolves account actions → 3) drafts replies → 4) escalates emotional/complex issues → 5) humans handle those.
usage: Replacement tools: LLM agents, Intercom Fin
future: Human fallback: Move to complex support, success management, escalations

name: Accounting & Audit
use: Advisory, forensic accounting, judgement calls
what: Transaction auditing and reporting — AI systems continuously audit 100% of records instead of sampling.
how: 1) Continuous data ingestion → 2) AI audits every transaction → 3) detects fraud patterns → 4) drafts reports → 5) auditors sign off & advise.
usage: Replacement tools: AI audit systems, Mindbridge
future: Human fallback: Advisory, forensic accounting, judgement calls

name: Paralegal / Legal Research
use: Move to litigation strategy, client counsel, court work
what: Document review and legal research — AI reads and summarises case law and contracts in seconds.
how: 1) AI ingests documents → 2) extracts clauses → 3) finds relevant precedent → 4) drafts summaries → 5) lawyers verify & strategise.
future: Human fallback: Move to litigation strategy, client counsel, court work

name: Translation (routine)
use: Specialise in localisation, literary, legal & live interpreting
what: Standard document translation — neural MT is near-human for common language pairs.
how: 1) AI translates draft → 2) checks terminology → 3) formats output → 4) human post-edits → 5) specialist handles legal/literary nuance.
future: Human fallback: Specialise in localisation, literary, legal & live interpreting

name: Radiology Image Reading
use: Focus on complex diagnosis, patient care, intervention
what: First-read of scans — AI flags anomalies with high accuracy, speeding diagnosis (radiologists still sign off).
how: 1) AI pre-reads scans → 2) highlights suspected findings → 3) prioritises urgent cases → 4) drafts report → 5) radiologist confirms & decides.
usage: Replacement tools: Aidoc, medical vision AI
future: Human fallback: Focus on complex diagnosis, patient care, intervention

name: Truck / Freight Driving
use: Move to logistics coordination, fleet ops, specialised haulage
what: Long-haul freight — autonomous fleets are advancing but face regulation, weather and edge-case hurdles, so this is a longer horizon.
how: 1) Highway auto-pilot → 2) depot-to-depot autonomy → 3) remote human oversight → 4) full corridor autonomy → 5) humans handle first/last mile & exceptions.
future: Human fallback: Move to logistics coordination, fleet ops, specialised haulage

name: Warehouse Picking
use: Robot supervision, maintenance, logistics planning
what: Manual order picking — robots handle more each year, though dexterity for varied items is still hard.
how: 1) Robots move shelves → 2) arms pick standard items → 3) vision handles variety → 4) humans pick odd items → 5) oversee & maintain robots.
future: Human fallback: Robot supervision, maintenance, logistics planning

name: Fast-Food Preparation
use: Move to hospitality, chef roles, experience-led venues
what: Repetitive food assembly — kitchen robots handle fixed menus, but flexible cooking stays hard.
how: 1) Robots fry/grill fixed items → 2) assemble standard orders → 3) humans handle custom/complex → 4) oversee machines → 5) focus on service.
future: Human fallback: Move to hospitality, chef roles, experience-led venues

name: Retail Cashier
use: Customer service, floor expertise, loss prevention
what: Checkout scanning — self-checkout and just-walk-out tech reduce cashier roles over time.
how: 1) Self-checkout added → 2) mobile scan-and-go → 3) camera-based auto-checkout → 4) humans assist & handle issues → 5) shift to service roles.
future: Human fallback: Customer service, floor expertise, loss prevention

name: Electrician
use: In demand — upskill with smart-home & solar/EV systems
what: Diagnosing and wiring in unpredictable real-world environments — high dexterity, judgement and safety stakes keep this firmly human.
how: AI can assist with diagnostics and design, but the physical, varied, safety-critical work resists automation for the foreseeable future.
future: Human fallback / how to stay ahead: In demand — upskill with smart-home & solar/EV systems

name: Plumber
use: Very safe — add gas, hydronics, green-plumbing skills
what: Working in cramped, non-standard spaces with constant problem-solving — physical dexterity robots can't match affordably.
how: Every job differs; the blend of manual skill, diagnosis and improvisation is exactly what automation struggles with.
future: Human fallback / how to stay ahead: Very safe — add gas, hydronics, green-plumbing skills

name: Therapist / Counsellor
use: Safe — human connection is the core value
what: Human empathy, trust and nuanced emotional judgement — people want a human in the room for mental health.
how: AI can offer support tools and triage, but genuine therapeutic connection and accountability remain human.
future: Human fallback / how to stay ahead: Safe — human connection is the core value

name: Nurse
use: High demand — tech-augmented, not replaced
what: Hands-on care, judgement under pressure and human reassurance — deeply physical and relational.
how: AI supports monitoring and admin, freeing nurses for care; the caring, adaptive human role stays central.
future: Human fallback / how to stay ahead: High demand — tech-augmented, not replaced

name: Teacher / Educator
use: Safe — shift toward mentoring & personalised guidance
what: Motivating, mentoring and adapting to real children — social and emotional skills AI can't replicate.
how: AI personalises materials and grades routine work, but inspiring and guiding learners is a human craft.
future: Human fallback / how to stay ahead: Safe — shift toward mentoring & personalised guidance

name: Emergency Responder
use: Safe — critical human judgement under pressure
what: Split-second decisions in chaotic, high-stakes, physical situations — human judgement and courage are irreplaceable.
how: Robots and AI assist (drones, triage tools), but people make the calls and do the rescuing.
future: Human fallback / how to stay ahead: Safe — critical human judgement under pressure

name: Creative Director / Strategist
use: Safe — those who direct AI well are more valuable
what: Original vision, taste and cross-domain strategy — AI generates options, but humans decide what matters and why.
how: AI is a powerful tool in the workflow; the directing taste, brand judgement and accountability stay human.
future: Human fallback / how to stay ahead: Safe — those who direct AI well are more valuable

name: Carpenter / Builder
use: Safe — add green-build & smart-construction skills
what: Building in varied, unpredictable real-world sites with craft and problem-solving — hard and costly to automate.
how: Prefab and tools improve productivity, but on-site adaptive craft remains human.
future: Human fallback / how to stay ahead: Safe — add green-build & smart-construction skills

name: Leadership & People Management
use: Safe — human leadership grows more valuable
what: Motivating people, building trust, navigating ethics and ambiguity — deeply human relational work.
how: AI informs decisions with data, but leading, inspiring and taking responsibility are human roles.
future: Human fallback / how to stay ahead: Safe — human leadership grows more valuable

name: Assembly Line Operator
use: Reskill into robotics maintenance or QA
what: Repetitive assembly tasks on production lines, increasingly handled by robotic cells.
how: Fixed robotic arms and vision systems perform assembly; humans handle setup and exceptions.

name: Crop Harvesting Labour
use: Move into agri-robotics operation and agronomy
what: Manual picking and field labour, targeted by autonomous harvesting robots.
how: Vision-guided robots identify and pick ripe produce; humans supervise fleets.

name: Pharma QC Lab Technician
use: Shift to method design and validation oversight
what: Routine quality-control testing in pharmaceutical labs, partly automated by robotic assays.
how: Automated liquid handlers and AI analysis run standardised tests; humans review anomalies.

name: Mining Equipment Operator
use: Retrain for remote-operations control rooms
what: Operating haul trucks and drills, increasingly autonomous at large mine sites.
how: GPS-guided autonomous vehicles run haulage; operators supervise remotely.
